notes.hypothesis: The probes should respond quickly when moved from submerged water to air/drying conditions, but the measured curve may include manual handling time, water clinging to probe faces, and probe-to-probe contact artifacts.
notes.method: All four probes started wet from the common water cup and were lifted out for a wet-to-dry transition capture at 0.5 s cadence. The probes were stuck to each other from being together in the cup; s1 and s2 in particular were delayed because the flat faces had to be physically separated by hand. This run is therefore a handling-included transition, not a fixture-controlled response curve.
notes.findings: All four channels moved strongly from wet toward dry. First-to-last raw ADC deltas were s1 +1843, s2 +1946, s3 +1920, s4 +1906 counts. End values reached about 2868-2926 raw ADC. The curve shows a rapid initial transition followed by slower settling.
notes.conclusion: The sensors respond fast enough for Sprout's plant-watering use case. More exact response-curve mapping would require a fixture and synchronized motion, but the added precision is not currently needed for bench decisions.
